title: Continue vs Ralph Workflow repo comparison page
why_now: current bottleneck is still repo conversion into Codeberg-first evaluation, not another same-family outreach burst
directory, curator, Apollo, and StackOverflow lanes are already inside cooldown or measurement windows
shared market intelligence already identified Continue as a real comparison surface
a Codeberg-first Continue comparison page can ship immediately and improve conversion for the traffic already being created

verification.command: python3 local link-existence check + git diff inspection

docs_review.what_changed: Added a new Continue comparison page and linked it from docs/README.md under Comparisons.
docs_review.why_here: This is a high-intent evaluator asset for repo visitors who are already comparing developer AI workflows. docs/README is the right discovery surface without bloating the top-level README route list.
docs_review.what_was_pruned_or_why_not: Nothing was removed because README and START_HERE were already short and clear; keeping them stable avoids turning top-level surfaces into a comparison link farm.
docs_review.duplication_reduced: The new page reuses existing first-task, unattended-fit, and review guides instead of scattering the same comparison framing across multiple surfaces.
docs_review.why_top_level_is_better: Continue-intent evaluators now have a direct Codeberg-first comparison path while the first-screen journey stays focused on start-here and first-task evaluation.

measurement_window.success_signal: page gets reused in follow-on distribution or comparison backlink execution and the broader Codeberg adoption window shows positive movement
measurement_window.replace_if_flat: ship a different bottom-funnel proof/comparison asset and pair it with a non-overlapping executable distribution lane
